div.p: Walter del Bois to all sons of holy mother Church who see or hear these letters : Greeting. Let them know that, for the health of my own soul, and of my father’s, and of my mother’s, and of Galiena my spouse’s, and of Richard my son’s, and of my ancestors’, and successors’, I have given, and granted, and by this present Charter have made firm to God and to Saint Cuthbert and the monks of Durham in pure and perpetual alms the land at ‘Tavenaus’ by these boundaries: that is, from the ‘Redehow’ on the ‘Kircelesbanc’ to the road that leads to ‘Roberdesbi’, and by that road northwards to the first syke that comes from the peat bog, and falls into the Kirtle, and by descending as far as ‘Redebanc’. Wherefore I will, and enjoin, that the aforesaid monks should have, and possess the whole land aforesaid within the aforenamed boundaries, in wood and plain, with common pasture of the toun of Carruthers for a hundred sheep, forty cattle, fifty goats, twenty pigs, and three horses, freely, quietly and honourably, from all custom or aid, and exaction, just as any Alms in the whole Kingdom is freely or quietly had, or possessed. With these witnesses : Richard my son; Galiena my spouse; Ralph of Wassand; Páitín the grieve; William d'Epaignes; Robert de Lithum; Adam son of Richard; Stephen the clerk; Hamo; Geoffrey son of Albelot; and many others.
